description: Environment variables implicitly granted to all MCP servers without requiring a capability token. Per protocol v0.1 §5.4. This list is deliberately conservative: only widely-relied-upon, non-secret variables are included. Anything that commonly carries credentials, tokens, or API keys is intentionally excluded — those require explicit 'secret:read:<NAME>' or 'env:read:<NAME>' grants.
notes: Implicitly-granted variables are passed to the MCP server's environment without prompting the user.

Variables matched by an 'explicitly_denied_patterns' glob are stripped from the environment unconditionally, even if the user attempts to override via policy. A future v0.2 MAY allow policy override under audit, but v0.1 keeps the deny absolute to make the threat model tractable.
Variables NOT in either list are denied by default. A server that needs them must declare 'env:read:<NAME>' in its manifest and have the capability granted.

Verifiers MUST refuse to launch a server when its inherited environment would include a denied-pattern variable that policy did not explicitly strip. This protects against accidental key leakage from the host shell.
Adding a variable to 'allowed' is a breaking change to the trust model and MUST go through a v0.x → v0.(x+1) version bump.
PWD and OLDPWD leak the current working directory path, which can be modestly sensitive. They are allowed because too many tools break without them; v0.2 may reconsider.
